title: To amend the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 to modify the railroad track maintenance credit.
stage_label: Referred to the House Committee on Ways and Means.
plain_summary: This bill makes changes to a tax credit that helps railroad companies pay for fixing and maintaining their train tracks. The tax credit amount increases from three thousand five hundred dollars to six thousand one hundred dollars for each mile of track. Starting in twenty twenty six, the credit amount will go up each year to keep up with inflation. The bill also lets railroad companies use this tax credit for tracks they owned as of twenty twenty four instead of only tracks they owned before twenty fifteen. These changes begin in twenty twenty five.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Ways and Means.

sections.title: Section 1  Modification of Railroad Track Maintenance Credit
sections.text: This section makes changes to the tax credit that railroad companies can claim for maintaining their tracks. First, it increases the credit amount from three thousand five hundred dollars to six thousand one hundred dollars per mile of track maintained. Second, it adds a rule that starting in twenty twenty six, this credit amount will increase each year based on inflation. The inflation increase will be calculated using the same method the government uses for other tax amounts, and any increase will be rounded to the nearest one hundred dollars. Third, it updates the starting date for what counts as qualified track maintenance spending from January first two thousand fifteen to January first two thousand twenty four. These changes will apply to money spent on track maintenance in tax years that begin after December thirty first two thousand twenty four.
